body:
This PR uses `OnCell` periodic boundary condition whenever possible. This is done by checking if all the dimensions are equispaced and if they are, ~~use `BSpline`~~ add virtual longitude points by repeating the first longitude slice.

~~All dimensions must be equispaced, because `BSpline` in Interpolations.jl required that. As a result, if the interpolation is from data with time, longitude, and latitude as the dimensions, then a `BSpline` cannot be used. As a result, the interpolation is not correct for the longitude dimension.~~

The proposed solution is to use a heuristic to automatically ~~choose oncell~~ add virtual longitude points if one of the dimensions span 360 degrees by seeing if `last(dim) - first(dim) + dim[2] - dim[1] ` is 360 degrees and the dimension is equispaced.

This is a stopgap solution to #181.

TODO

- [X] Add tests
title: Use oncell periodic condition when possible
